title: Directors cuts cut for a reason
display_title: Directors cuts cut for a reason
wikitext:
[]: Director's cuts: cut for a reason?             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Fri Nov 28, 2008 7:50 pm One of the greatest apparent plot holes in the series is the whole why-was-Kaworu-surprised-to-see-Lilith issue, but is it possible that the scene in 24' that shows that Kaworu knows Gendo possesses Adam's body was cut in order to alleviate potential confusion? In other words, is it possible that the Kaworu/SEELE meeting was removed from the final cut because it created such a plot hole? Basic question: were all such scenes cut for timing reasons? In the case of the Kaworu scene, they could have cut the scene were Eva 01 sits with Kaworu in hand for a mind-numbingly long period of time shorter if the Kaworu/SEELE meeting was truly important to the plot. Remove that scene from the equation, and Kaworu's confusion discovering Lilith makes sense. Are all scenes included in the director's cut episodes considered 100% canon, even when the plot makes more sense without them?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Nov 28, 2008 7:57 pm The additional scenes from the director's cuts episodes were made AFTER the show aired. Nothing was "cut" from the original.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 28, 2008 8:02 pm Although, for a bit of trivia, the scene outside the Artificial Evolution Laboratory where Yui and Fuyutsuki are reunited was in the storyboards from the start. So that's possibly the only one that sort of counts as "cut, then put back in".

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Nov 29, 2008 12:01 pm the answer is simple: MONEY even stuff that might not have been "in the storyboards", was arguably still "intended" (in some shape or form) but they ran out of money to physically make them (incidentally, they were also running out of time to do it) but it wasn't cut to make the episodes shorter That's why the Director's Cuts are generally considered the "definitive version": the originals were blatantly suffering from budget problems and not what they "wanted" to make

[]:             Quote Post by Melchior » Sat Nov 29, 2008 10:34 pm Another thing to take into account... Anno never intended on making the EoE movie. It was the original 26 episodes, and then because of the hate mails, he made EoE. The Kaworu part was suppose to be as short as the rest of the other angels. The idea might have been in Anno's head from the beginning to make EoE, but, until practically forced to make it, there was no reason to know as much as we now do about Kaworu...which in all honesty still isn't that much. ...and yes, I agree budget played a role in it as well. I'm hoping Rebuild with it's limitless budget will be a little more defining.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 29, 2008 10:39 pm Melchior wrote: then because of the hate mails , he made EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Sat Nov 29, 2008 10:49 pm Melchior wrote: Another thing to take into account... Anno never intended on making the EoE movie. It was the original 26 episodes, and then because of the hate mails, he made EoE. The Kaworu part was suppose to be as short as the rest of the other angels. The idea might have been in Anno's head from the beginning to make EoE, but, until practically forced to make it, there was no reason to know as much as we now do about Kaworu...which in all honesty still isn't that much. ...and yes, I agree budget played a role in it as well. I'm hoping Rebuild with it's limitless budget will be a little more defining.

[]:             Quote Post by Melchior » Sat Nov 29, 2008 10:54 pm Reichu wrote: Melchior wrote: then because of the hate mails , he made EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Nov 30, 2008 12:45 am Melchior wrote: No saucy for the first part, It's just the common ground for a lot of people. I mean, why make one ending if you're planning another. Just make the one ending and save yourself the money.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 30, 2008 4:37 am The Japanese equivalent of Platinum included a set of animatic rushes for what would have been episode 25, from which this iconic image is taken There are also other references to events that only make sense if EoE is already unfolding in the physical world are laced throughout the episode 25 we did eventually see: 6m12s : EVA-02 in the lake 6m21s : Asuka feels useless at the controls 10m38s : Ritsuko in the LCL 10m43s : Misato KIA

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 30, 2008 11:26 am Legendary wrote: They planned the EoE ending for a good period, the Episode 25 preview shows the MP Evas at least, and I think Misato shooting the guard.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 30, 2008 12:59 pm Reichu wrote: What about Rei and Gendo, and Shinji's encounter with Eva-01?

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sun Nov 30, 2008 9:00 pm Mr. Tines wrote: 6m21s : Asuka feels useless at the controls

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 30, 2008 10:40 pm Yeah, it's recycled. But the background is blue because, well, Eva-02 is underwater in that not-so-recycled shot.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Dec 01, 2008 12:16 am Mr. Tines wrote: The Japanese equivalent of Platinum included a set of animatic rushes for what would have been episode 25, from which this iconic image is taken http://www.stevegilham.com/nge/Ep25Air-Splat.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 01, 2008 12:24 am V wrote: Do you have any more of these? (i.e. the ones of the MP Eva fight?)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Dec 01, 2008 12:29 am now I've been meaning to ask this: that's the "next episode preview" that aired live on TV in Japan in the initial run....? BUT....it was not included on the DVD version's "next episode preview" for either English OR Japanese release? (i.e. the Renewal episode 24' does not contain this as the "next episode" thing?) (if they're on that video, when does the "next episode" preview start?)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 01, 2008 12:38 am Episode 24 = On Air Version: Contains preview for Episode 25 (The World Ending/Do You Love Me?) Episode 24' = "Director's Cut": Contains preview for Episode 25' (Air/Love is Destructive) And the preview is shown right after the credits...

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Dec 01, 2008 2:07 am Unsauced assertion here, which Reichu will probably jump all over like a hyena in heat, but I believe that what became EoE was already in the works to air as episodes 25 and 26, but after Kaworu got his head popped off, the TV stations forced the crew to do a completely new ending scenario, with which the two episodes we know and love were made. Some stuff which they already had done ended up there, but for the most part, what we see is entirely different from what was originally planned. (a somewhat tame ending compared to the grisly bad end we get in EoE) This is all IIRC from something I read somewhere, as mentioned. At the moment, I think delicious sauce might be found in that one document that supposedly came from a Gainax employee that was posted on Japanese boards. As I don't have a link handy, or even know the reliabilty, take everything in the above paragraph with a grain of salt.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 01, 2008 2:19 am CorporalChaos wrote: This is all IIRC from something I read somewhere
